success_surface: Formula-based cell manipulation with conditional logic across multiple rows
effective_behaviors: Used openpyxl library for formula manipulation as prescribed by skill guide
Verified sheet availability before access via programmatic listing
Parsed target cell range specification into iteratable row indices
Constructed Excel formulas as raw strings with dynamic cell references per row
Applied formulas individually to each target cell maintaining relative references
Preserved original file by writing to separate output path
Reloaded saved file to verify formulas were correctly persisted
Logged applied formulas and verification results for transparency
evidence: print(f"Available sheets: {wb.sheetnames}") confirmed sheet presence before access
for row_num in range(2, 6): properly converted D2:D5 range to row iteration
formula = f'=B{row_num}+C{row_num}+IF(A{row_num}="A",10,...)' used dynamic references per row
ws[cell_address].value = formula set formulas as raw strings not evaluated values
wb.save(output_path) wrote to new path preserving original input
wb_check = load_workbook(output_path); ws_check[...] verified formulas persisted correctly

memory_items.title: Conditional Formula Construction
memory_items.lesson: When building Excel formulas programmatically, construct them as raw string templates with dynamic cell references that adjust per row
memory_items.generalization: Use f-string formatting to inject row/column variables into formula strings (e.g., 'IF(A{row},10,-10)') ensuring each cell gets its own relative reference rather than static absolute references

memory_items.title: Target Range Iteration
memory_items.lesson: Convert specified cell ranges into iterable loops rather than hardcoding individual cell addresses
memory_items.generalization: Parse range specifications (like D2:D5) by extracting start/end row numbers and iterating through the sequence, constructing cell addresses dynamically for each iteration

memory_items.title: Post-Save Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving workbook changes, reload the output file to verify modifications persisted correctly
memory_items.generalization: Perform a read-back validation pass by loading the saved file and checking key cells contain expected values or formulas, catching write errors before task completion

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Implementation Patterns
- **Conditional Logic**: Build Excel formulas with nested functions (IF, SUM, etc.) as string templates using dynamic cell references. Format: f'=FUNCTION({cell_ref},{params})' where cell_ref adjusts per row.
- **Multi-Cell Application**: When applying formulas across a range, iterate through rows/columns programmatically rather than hardcoding individual addresses. Extract start/end coordinates from target range specification.
- **Verification Loop**: After saving modified workbooks, reload the output file to confirm formulas persisted as raw formula strings (not evaluated values). Check representative cells match expected formula structure.
